meaning: n.举行赛会的场所,露天市场
examples: It was no more than a passing gimmick or a fairground attraction.
Michael came on, and that voice of his rang over the whole fairground.
Measuring appeal is as exact a science as guessing the number of sweets in a jar at the fairground.
The show, which dates back to 1847, hosts livestock, grandstand performances, trade stands and fairground attractions.
West Berlin, meanwhile, had transformed itself into a giant fairground, and the Wall was its biggest ride.
